questions.q: In which primary art form did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam, known as the 'Voice Of The South', make his career?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam was primarily a playback singer, recording songs for films across many Indian languages.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam is most strongly associated with the film industries of which region of India?
questions.explanation: Nicknamed the 'Voice Of The South', SPB was a dominant playback voice in Telugu, Tamil and Kannada cinema, among others.

questions.q: In which year was the playback singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam born?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam was born in 1946 in the Nellore district of present-day Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: In which year did the legendary singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam pass away?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam died in September 2020 from complications of COVID-19.

questions.q: Besides being a playback singer, in which other roles did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam also work in cinema?
questions.explanation: Apart from singing, SPB also worked as a music composer (music director) and acted in films.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam is widely cited as being among the most prolific singers in cinema chiefly because of what?
options: The vast number of songs he recorded
The number of countries he toured
questions.explanation: SPB is renowned for recording an extraordinarily large number of songs over his career, making him one of the most prolific.

questions.q: Which of these languages did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam famously sing in?
options: Telugu, Tamil, Kannada, Hindi and others
questions.explanation: SPB recorded songs in many languages including Telugu, Tamil, Kannada, Hindi and Malayalam.

questions.q: What is the commonly used short form by which the singer S. P. Balasubrahmanyam is affectionately known?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam is widely and affectionately referred to by the initials 'SPB'.

questions.q: Which prestigious civilian honour was awarded to S. P. Balasubrahmanyam by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: SPB was honoured with the Padma Bhushan (2011), one of India's high civilian awards; he later received the Padma Vibhushan posthumously.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam win during his career?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam won six National Film Awards for Best Male Playback Singer across multiple languages.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam recorded a vast number of songs in collaboration with which celebrated South Indian music composer in particular?
questions.explanation: SPB had a hugely prolific partnership with composer Ilaiyaraaja, recording numerous songs together.

questions.q: In 1989, S. P. Balasubrahmanyam sang the Hindi playback for Salman Khan's debut as a leading man in which blockbuster film?
questions.explanation: SPB sang the songs for Salman Khan in 'Maine Pyar Kiya' (1989), composed by Raamlaxman, which brought him wide popularity in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam made his playback singing debut in the year 1966 in which language film industry?
questions.explanation: SPB made his playback debut in 1966 in Telugu cinema with the film 'Sri Sri Sri Maryada Ramanna'.

questions.q: Which music composer gave S. P. Balasubrahmanyam his first break as a playback singer in 1966?
questions.explanation: Composer S. P. Kodandapani gave SPB his debut break in the 1966 Telugu film 'Sri Sri Sri Maryada Ramanna'.

questions.q: For which 1980 Telugu film, centred on a Carnatic vocalist, did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam win his first National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer?
questions.explanation: SPB won his first National Film Award for the classical-music-centred Telugu film 'Sankarabharanam', composed by K. V. Mahadevan.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam provided the Tamil playback voice for many films of which superstar actor closely linked with his songs?
questions.explanation: SPB famously lent his Tamil playback voice to films of both Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan, among others.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam frequently dubbed the Tamil and Telugu speaking voice for which actor in his films?
questions.explanation: SPB often served as the dubbing voice for actor Kamal Haasan in several Tamil and Telugu films.

questions.q: In the 1992 film 'Roja', S. P. Balasubrahmanyam worked with which then-emerging composer who later won global acclaim?
questions.explanation: SPB sang in A. R. Rahman's debut film 'Roja' (1992), at the start of Rahman's celebrated career.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam is reported to hold a Guinness World Record for which singing-related achievement?
options: Most concerts in a single city
Most film awards in one year
Recording the most songs by an individual singer
questions.explanation: SPB is widely cited in Guinness World Records for recording the most songs by an individual singer, with tens of thousands of recordings across languages.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam was famous for his versatility across which range of song styles?
options: Romantic, devotional, classical and comedy songs
questions.explanation: SPB was celebrated for handling romantic, devotional, classical and even comedy numbers with equal ease.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam hosted and mentored aspiring singers on television music reality shows in which primary language?
questions.explanation: SPB hosted and mentored on Telugu-language singing reality shows such as 'Paadutha Theeyaga', encouraging many young singers.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam's son, who followed him into the music industry as a playback singer and film producer, is known by what name?
questions.explanation: His son S. P. Charan is also a playback singer and film producer, while his sister S. P. Sailaja is a singer too.

questions.q: For which 1983 Telugu film, centred on two classical dancers, did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam win a National Film Award singing for composer Ilaiyaraaja?
questions.explanation: SPB won a National Film Award for the Ilaiyaraaja-composed Telugu film 'Sagara Sangamam' (1983).

questions.q: Which composer, who scored the Salman Khan hits 'Maine Pyar Kiya' and 'Hum Aapke Hain Koun..!', frequently used S. P. Balasubrahmanyam as his playback voice in Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: SPB worked extensively with composer Raamlaxman, notably on Rajshri films like 'Maine Pyar Kiya' (1989) and 'Hum Aapke Hain Koun..!' (1994).

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam reportedly set a notable record by recording around 21 songs in a single day. In which language was this widely reported feat associated?
questions.explanation: SPB is reported to have recorded about 21 Kannada songs in a single day, illustrating his prolific output.

questions.q: Before fully committing to a singing career, S. P. Balasubrahmanyam initially trained in which professional field?
questions.explanation: SPB had enrolled to study engineering before pursuing music full-time as his career.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam's full given name reflects his family name; his initials 'S. P.' stand for which of the following?
questions.explanation: The initials 'S. P.' come from his family name 'Sripathi Panditaradhyula', preceding Balasubrahmanyam.

questions.q: For which 1981 Hindi cross-cultural love story, composed by Laxmikant-Pyarelal, did S. P. Balasubrahmanyam win his National Film Award for a Hindi song?
questions.explanation: SPB won the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer for the Hindi film 'Ek Duuje Ke Liye' (1981), composed by Laxmikant-Pyarelal.

questions.q: In which year was S. P. Balasubrahmanyam hospitalised after testing positive for COVID-19, the illness that led to his death?
questions.explanation: SPB was hospitalised in August 2020 after testing positive for COVID-19, and died in September of that year.

questions.q: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam holds a place in Indian cinema history largely cited for which distinction?
options: Directing the most films in a year
Composing the first Indian film score
Being among the most prolific singers by number of recorded songs
questions.explanation: SPB is widely cited among the most prolific singers in cinema for the sheer number of songs he recorded across languages.
